statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.UnionAllTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, Segment) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'red' FROM bronze.Product WHERE Color = 'Red' UNION ALL SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'other' FROM bronze.Product WHERE Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'red' AS Segment FROM [bronze].[Product] WHERE Color = 'Red' UNION ALL SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'other' FROM [bronze].[Product] WHERE Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH source_product AS ( SELECT * FROM [bronze].[Product] ), red_products AS ( SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'red' AS Segment FROM source_product WHERE Color = 'Red' ), other_products AS ( SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'other' AS Segment FROM source_product WHERE Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL ), final AS ( SELECT * FROM red_products UNION ALL SELECT * FROM other_products ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both queries source from [bronze].[Product]. Extracted uses direct table reference; refactored wraps it in source_product CTE. Semantically equivalent.

output_columns.summary: Both queries output three columns: ProductAlternateKey (NVARCHAR(25)), EnglishProductName (NVARCHAR(50)), Segment (NVARCHAR(20)). Column definitions and casts are identical.

joins.summary: No joins in either query. Both operate on a single source table.

filters.summary: Extracted applies two filters: Color='Red' and Color<>'Red' OR Color IS NULL across UNION ALL branches. Refactored applies identical filters in red_products and other_products CTEs. Logically equivalent.

aggregation_grain.summary: No aggregation in either query. Both return row-level results combined with UNION ALL, maintaining the same grain.
